passage.text:
The History of Glass
From our earliest origins, man has been making use of glass. Historians have discovered that a type of natural glass – obsidian – formed in places such as the mouth of a volcano as a result of the intense heat of an eruption melting sand – was first used as tips for spears. Archaeologists have even found evidence of man-made glass which dates back to 4000 BC; this took the form of glazes used for coating stone beads. It was not until 1500 BC, however, that the first hollow glass container was made by covering a sand core with a layer of molten glass.
Glass blowing became the most common way to make glass containers from the first century BC. The glass made during this time was highly coloured due to the impurities of the raw material. In the first century AD, methods of creating colourless glass were developed, which was then tinted by the addition of colouring materials. The secret of glass making was taken across Europe by the Romans during this century. However, they guarded the skills and technology required to make glass very closely, and it was not until their empire collapsed in 476 AD that glass-making knowledge became widespread throughout Europe and the Middle East. From the 10th century onwards, the Venetians gained a reputation for technical skill and artistic ability in the making of glass bottles, and many of the city’s craftsmen left Italy to set up glassworks throughout Europe.
A major milestone in the history of glass occurred with the invention of lead crystal glass by the English glass manufacturer George Ravenscroft (1632-1683). He attempted to counter the effect of clouding that sometimes occurred in blown glass by introducing lead to the raw materials used in the process. The new glass he created was softer and easier to decorate, and had a higher refractive index, adding to its brilliance and beauty, and it proved invaluable to the optical industry. It is thanks to Ravenscroft’s invention that optical lenses, astronomical telescopes, microscopes and the like became possible.
In Britain, the modern glass industry only really started to develop after the repeal of the Excise Act in 1845. Before that time, heavy taxes had been placed on the amount of glass melted in a glasshouse, and were levied continuously from 1745 to 1845.Joseph Paxton’s Crystal Palace at London’s Great Exhibition of 1851 marked the beginning of glass as a material used in the building industry. This revolutionary new building encouraged the use of glass in public, domestic and horticultural architecture. Glass manufacturing techniques also improved with the advancement of science and the development of better technology.
From 1887 onwards, glass making developed from traditional mouth-blowing to a semi-automatic process, after factory-owner HM Ashley introduced a machine capable of producing 200 bottles per hour in Castleford, Yorkshire, England – more than three times quicker than any previous production method. Then in 1907, the first fully automated machine was developed in the USA by Michael Owens – founder of the Owens Bottle Machine Company (later the major manufacturers Owens-Illinois) – and installed in its factory. Owens’ invention could produce an impressive 2,500 bottles per hour. Other developments followed rapidly, but it was not until the First World War, when Britain became out off from essential glass suppliers, that glass became part of the scientific sector. Previous to this, glass had been as a craft rather than a precise science.
Today, glass making is big business. It has become a modern, hi-tech industry operating in a fiercely competitive global market where quality, design and service levels are critical to maintaining market share. Modern glass plants are capable of making millions of glass containers a day in many different colours, with green, brown and clear remaining the most popular. Few of us can imagine modern life without glass. It features in almost every aspect of our lives – in our homes, our cars and whenever we sit down to eat or drink. Glass packaging is used for many products, many beverages are sold in glass, as are numerous foodstuffs, as well as medicines and cosmetics.
Glass is an ideal material for recycling, and with growing consumer concern for green issues, glass bottles and jars are becoming ever more popular. Glass recycling is good news for the environment. It saves used glass containers being sent to landfill. As less energy is needed to melt recycled glass than to melt down raw materials, this also saves fuel and production costs. Recycling also reduces the need for raw materials to be quarried, thus saving precious resources.

questionGroups.instruction:
Complete the notes below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
questionGroups.note:
• Early humans used a material called [1] to make the sharp points of their [2].
• 4000 BC: [3] made of stone were covered in a coating of man-made glass.
• First century BC: glass was coloured because of the [4] in the material.
• Until 476 AD: Only the [5] knew how to make glass.
• From 10th century: Venetians became famous for making bottles out of glass.
• 17th century: George Ravenscroft developed a process using [6] to avoid the occurrence of [7] in blown glass.
• Mid-19th century: British glass production developed after changes to laws concerning [8].

questions.prompt: Early humans used a material called ___________ to make the sharp points of their ___ (2).
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Early humans, material, sharp points, spears.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn 1: "Historians have discovered that a type of natural glass – obsidian... was first used as tips for spears."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Sharp points" (đầu nhọn) đồng nghĩa với "tips".

"Early humans" tương ứng với việc con người ở thời kỳ đầu ("earliest origins").

Vật liệu được dùng là "obsidian" để làm đầu nhọn cho "spears".

Đáp án: obsidian

questions.prompt: (answer for sharp points)
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Early humans, material, sharp points, spears.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn 1: "Historians have discovered that a type of natural glass – obsidian... was first used as tips for spears."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Sharp points" (đầu nhọn) đồng nghĩa với "tips".

"Early humans" tương ứng với việc con người ở thời kỳ đầu ("earliest origins").

Vật liệu được dùng là "obsidian" để làm đầu nhọn cho "spears".

Đáp án: spears

questions.prompt: 4000 BC: ___________ made of stone were covered in a coating of man-made glass.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

4000 BC, stone, covered, coating, man-made glass.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn 1: "...man-made glass which dates back to 4000 BC; this took the form of glazes used for coating stone beads."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Covered in a coating" đồng nghĩa với "glazes used for coating".

Vật thể làm bằng đá ("stone") được phủ lớp này chính là "beads" (hạt chuỗi).

Đáp án: beads

questions.prompt: First century BC: glass was coloured because of the ___________ in the material.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

First century BC, coloured, material.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn 2: "The glass made during this time [first century BC] was highly coloured due to the impurities of the raw material."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Coloured because of" tương ứng với "coloured due to".

Nguyên nhân khiến thủy tinh bị đổi màu là do các tạp chất ("impurities") có trong nguyên liệu.

Đáp án: impurities

questions.prompt: Until 476 AD: Only the ___________ knew how to make glass.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Until 476 AD, Only, knew how to make glass.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn 2: "The secret of glass making was taken across Europe by the Romans... they guarded the skills... closely, and it was not until their empire collapsed in 476 AD that glass-making knowledge became widespread..."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Guarded the skills very closely" (giữ bí mật công nghệ rất kỹ) và chỉ lan rộng sau năm 476 AD nghĩa là trước mốc thời gian này, chỉ có người La Mã ("Romans") biết cách làm.

Đáp án: Romans

questions.prompt: 17th century: George Ravenscroft developed a process using ___________ to avoid the occurrence of ___ (7) in blown glass.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

17th century, George Ravenscroft, process, avoid, blown glass.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn 3: "George Ravenscroft (1632-1683). He attempted to counter the effect of clouding that sometimes occurred in blown glass by introducing lead to the raw materials..."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"17th century" ứng với những năm (1632-1683).

"Avoid" đồng nghĩa với "counter the effect of" (chống lại/ngăn chặn tác động).

Ông đã sử dụng "lead" (chì) để ngăn hiện tượng "clouding" (mờ đục).

Đáp án: lead

questions.prompt: (answer for occurrence of)
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

17th century, George Ravenscroft, process, avoid, blown glass.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn 3: "George Ravenscroft (1632-1683). He attempted to counter the effect of clouding that sometimes occurred in blown glass by introducing lead to the raw materials..."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"17th century" ứng với những năm (1632-1683).

"Avoid" đồng nghĩa với "counter the effect of" (chống lại/ngăn chặn tác động).

Ông đã sử dụng "lead" (chì) để ngăn hiện tượng "clouding" (mờ đục).

Đáp án: clouding

questions.prompt: Mid-19th century: British glass production developed after changes to laws concerning __________.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Mid-19th century, British, developed, changes to laws.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn 4: "In Britain, the modern glass industry only really started to develop after the repeal of the Excise Act in 1845. Before that time, heavy taxes had been placed..."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Mid-19th century" ứng với năm 1845.

"Repeal of the Excise Act" (bãi bỏ Đạo luật Thuế tiêu thụ đặc biệt) chính là sự thay đổi về luật pháp liên quan đến thuế ("taxes").

Đáp án: taxes

questionGroups.instruction:
Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage?
In boxes 9–13 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE  if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE  if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN  if there is no information on this

questions.prompt: In 1887, HM Ashley had the fastest bottle-producing machine that existed at the time.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

1887, HM Ashley, fastest, existed at the time.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn 5: "From 1887 onwards... HM Ashley introduced a machine... more than three times quicker than any previous production method."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Bài đọc khẳng định máy của Ashley nhanh gấp 3 lần "bất kỳ phương pháp sản xuất nào trước đó" ("quicker than any previous production method").

Điều này đồng nghĩa với việc tại thời điểm đó (1887), nó là cỗ máy nhanh nhất. Thông tin hoàn toàn trùng khớp.

Đáp án: TRUE

questions.prompt: Michael Owens was hired by a large US company to design a fully-automated bottle manufacturing machine for them.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Michael Owens, hired (được thuê), large US company, design (thiết kế).

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn 5: "...the first fully automated machine was developed in the USA by Michael Owens – founder of the Owens Bottle Machine Company (later the major manufacturers Owens-Illinois)..."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Câu hỏi đưa ra thông tin: Michael Owens được thuê bởi một công ty lớn của Mỹ để thiết kế máy cho họ.

Bài đọc cung cấp thông tin: Michael Owens chính là người sáng lập (founder) ra công ty Owens Bottle Machine Company (sau này trở thành tập đoàn sản xuất lớn Owens-Illinois).

Bản chất mâu thuẫn: Một người là "chủ/người sáng lập" tự phát triển máy cho công ty của chính mình, hoàn toàn trái ngược (mâu thuẫn) với việc ông ta là một "người làm thuê" được tuyển dụng về để thiết kế.

Do có sự mâu thuẫn trực tiếp về vai trò của đối tượng (Founder vs Hired Employee) nên câu này chúng ta chọn FALSE.

questions.prompt: Nowadays, most glass is produced by large international manufacturers.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Nowadays, most glass, produced, large international manufacturers.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn 6: "Today, glass making is big business. It has become a modern, hi-tech industry operating in a fiercely competitive global market..."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Bài đọc chỉ nói ngành này hoạt động trên thị trường toàn cầu cạnh tranh khốc liệt ("global market") và là ngành kinh doanh lớn ("big business"), nhưng không có số liệu hay khẳng định nào cho thấy "phần lớn thủy tinh" (most glass) được sản xuất bởi các "tập đoàn quốc tế lớn".

Không có thông tin so sánh lượng sản xuất của các công ty lớn → NOT GIVEN.

Đáp án: NOT GIVEN

questions.prompt: Concern for the environment is leading to an increased demand for glass containers.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Concern for the environment, increased demand, glass containers.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn 7: "and with growing consumer concern for green issues, glass bottles and jars are becoming ever more popular."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Concern for green issues" đồng nghĩa với "concern for the environment".

"Becoming ever more popular" (ngày càng phổ biến rộng rãi) đồng nghĩa với "increased demand" (nhu cầu tăng cao). Thông tin hoàn toàn trùng khớp.

Đáp án: TRUE

questions.prompt: It is more expensive to produce recycled glass than to manufacture new glass.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

More expensive, recycled glass, manufacture new glass.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn 7: "As less energy is needed to melt recycled glass than to melt down raw materials, this also saves fuel and production costs."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Bài đọc nói việc nung thủy tinh tái chế tốn ít năng lượng hơn, giúp "tiết kiệm nhiên liệu và chi phí sản xuất" ("saves fuel and production costs").

Nghĩa là sản xuất thủy tinh tái chế rẻ hơn (less expensive), ngược lại với khẳng định đắt hơn (more expensive) của câu hỏi.

Đáp án: FALSE

passage.text:
Bring back the big cats
It’s time to start returning vanished native animals to Britain, says John Vesty
There is a poem, written around 598 AD, which describes hunting a mystery animal called a llewyn. But what was it? Nothing seemed to fit, until 2006, when an animal bone, dating from around the same period, was found in the Kinsey Cave in northern England. Until this discovery, the lynx – a large spotted cat with tasselled ears – was presumed to have died out in Britain at least 6,000 years ago, before the inhabitants of these islands took up farming. But the 2006 find, together with three others in Yorkshire and Scotland, is compelling evidence that the lynx and the mysterious llewyn were in fact one and the same animal. If this is so, it would bring forward the tassel-eared cat’s estimated extinction date by roughly 5,000 years.
However, this is not quite the last glimpse of the animal in British culture. A 9th-century stone cross from the Isle of Eigg shows, alongside the deer, boar and aurochs pursued by a mounted hunter, a speckled cat with tasselled ears. Were it not for the animal’s backside having worn away with time, we could have been certain, as the lynx’s stubby tail is unmistakable. But even without this key feature, it’s hard to see what else the creature could have been. The lynx is now becoming the totemic animal of a movement that is transforming British environmentalism: rewilding.
Rewilding means the mass restoration of damaged ecosystems. It involves letting trees return to places that have been denuded, allowing parts of the seabed to recover from trawling and dredging, permitting rivers to flow freely again. Above all, it means bringing back missing species. One of the most striking findings of modern ecology is that ecosystems without large predators behave in completely different ways from those that retain them. Some of them drive dynamic processes that resonate through the whole food chain, creating niches for hundreds of species that might otherwise struggle to survive. The killers turn out to be bringers of life.
Such findings present a big challenge to British conservation, which has often selected arbitrary assemblages of plants and animals and sought, at great effort and expense, to prevent them from changing. It has tried to preserve the living world as if it were a jar of pickles, letting nothing in and nothing out, keeping nature in a state of arrested development. But ecosystems are not merely collections of species; they are also the dynamic and ever-shifting relationships between them. And this dynamism often depends on large predators.
At sea the potential is even greater: by protecting large areas from commercial fishing, we could once more see what 18th-century literature describes: vast shoals of fish being chased by fin and sperm whales, within sight of the English shore. This policy would also greatly boost catches in the surrounding seas; the fishing industry’s insistence on scouring every inch of seabed, leaving no breeding reserves, could not be more damaging to its own interests.
Rewilding is a rare example of an environmental movement in which campaigners articulate what they are for rather than only what they are against. One of the reasons why the enthusiasm for rewilding is spreading so quickly in Britain is that it helps to create a more inspiring vision than the green movement’s usual promise of ‘Follow us and the world will be slightly less awful than it would otherwise have been.’
The lynx presents no threat to human beings: there is no known instance of one preying on people. It is a specialist predator of roe deer, a species that has exploded in Britain in recent decades, holding back, by intensive browsing, attempts to re-establish forests. It will also winkle out sika deer: an exotic species that is almost impossible for human beings to control, as it hides in impenetrable plantations of young trees. The attempt to reintroduce this predator marries well with the aim of bringing forests back to parts of our bare and barren uplands. The lynx requires deep cover, and as such presents little risk to sheep and other livestock, which are supposed, as a condition of farm subsidies, to be kept out of the woods.
On a recent trip to the Cairngorm Mountains, I heard several conservationists suggest that the lynx could be reintroduced there within 20 years. If trees return to the bare hills elsewhere in Britain, the big cats could soon follow. There is nothing extraordinary about these proposals, seen from the perspective of anywhere else in Europe. The lynx has now been reintroduced to the Jura Mountains, the Alps, the Vosges in eastern France and the Harz mountains in Germany, and has re-established itself in many more places. The European population has tripled since 1970 to roughly 10,000. As with wolves, bears, beavers, boar, bison, moose and many other species, the lynx has been able to spread as farming has left the hills and people discover that it is more lucrative to protect charismatic wildlife than to hunt it, as tourists will pay for the chance to see it. Large-scale rewilding is happening almost everywhere – except Britain.
Here, attitudes are just beginning to change. Conservationists are starting to accept that the old preservation-jar model is failing, even on its own terms. Already, projects such as Trees for Life in the Highlands provide a hint of what might be coming. An organisation is being set up that will seek to catalyse the rewilding of land and sea across Britain, its aim being to reintroduce that rarest of species to British ecosystems: hope.

questions.prompt: What did the 2006 discovery of the animal bone reveal about the lynx?

options.label: A. Its physical appearance was very distinctive.
B. Its extinction was linked to the spread of farming.
C. It vanished from Britain several thousand years ago.
D. It survived in Britain longer than was previously thought.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

2006 discovery, animal bone, reveal, lynx.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn 1: "Until this discovery, the lynx... was presumed to have died out in Britain at least 6,000 years ago... But the 2006 find... is compelling evidence that... would bring forward the tassel-eared cat’s estimated extinction date by roughly 5,000 years."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Trước phát hiện năm 2006, người ta nghĩ loài linh miêu đã tuyệt chủng cách đây 6,000 năm. Nhưng phát hiện mới dời mốc thời gian này lại gần hơn 5,000 năm (tức là chúng mới tuyệt chủng cách đây khoảng 1,000 năm).

Điều này chứng minh loài linh miêu đã sống sót ở Anh lâu hơn so với những gì người ta từng nghĩ trước đó ("survived in Britain longer than was previously thought").

Đáp án: D

questions.prompt: What point does the writer make about large predators in the third paragraph?

options.label: A. Their presence can increase biodiversity.
B. They may cause damage to local ecosystems.
C. Their behaviour can alter according to the environment.
D. They should be reintroduced only to areas where they were native.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Large predators, third paragraph.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn 3: "...ecosystems without large predators behave in completely different ways... Some of them drive dynamic processes... creating niches for hundreds of species that might otherwise struggle to survive. The killers turn out to be bringers of life."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Việc các loài thú săn mồi lớn tạo ra môi trường sống (niches) cho hàng trăm loài khác sinh tồn thể hiện rằng sự có mặt của chúng giúp tăng cường đa dạng sinh học ("increase biodiversity").

Đáp án: A

questions.prompt: What does the writer suggest about British conservation in the fourth paragraph?

options.label: A. It has failed to achieve its aims.
B. It is beginning to change direction.
C. It has taken a misguided approach.
D. It has focused on the most widespread species.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

British conservation, fourth paragraph.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn 4: "It has tried to preserve the living world as if it were a jar of pickles, letting nothing in and nothing out, keeping nature in a state of arrested development."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Tác giả chỉ trích xu hướng bảo tồn của Anh giống như việc giữ một "hũ dưa muối", giữ tự nhiên ở trạng thái ngưng đọng, không thay đổi, trong khi hệ sinh thái cần sự vận động và dịch chuyển liên tục.

Do đó, đây là một cách tiếp cận sai lầm, sai hướng ("a misguided approach").

Đáp án: C

questions.prompt: Protecting large areas of the sea from commercial fishing would result in

options.label: A. practical benefits for the fishing industry.
B. some short-term losses to the fishing industry.
C. widespread opposition from the fishing industry.
D. certain changes to techniques within the fishing industry.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Protecting, sea, commercial fishing, result in.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn 5: "This policy would also greatly boost catches in the surrounding seas; the fishing industry’s insistence on scouring every inch of seabed... could not be more damaging to its own interests."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Việc bảo vệ các vùng biển khỏi khai thác thương mại sẽ giúp tăng mạnh lượng cá đánh bắt ở các vùng biển xung quanh ("boost catches"). Tác giả nhấn mạnh việc tận diệt của ngành cá hiện tại đang tự gây hại cho chính họ.

Vì vậy, chính sách bảo vệ này mang lại lợi ích thực tế cho chính ngành đánh cá ("practical benefits for the fishing industry").

Đáp án: A

questions.prompt: According to the author, what distinguishes rewilding from other environmental campaigns?

options.label: A. Its objective is more achievable.
B. Its supporters are more articulate.
C. Its positive message is more appealing.
D. It is based on sounder scientific principles.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Distinguishes, rewilding, other environmental campaigns.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn 6: "Rewilding is a rare example of an environmental movement in which campaigners articulate what they are for rather than only what they are against... it helps to create a more inspiring vision than the green movement’s usual promise..."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Phong trào "rewilding" tập trung vào những điều họ ủng hộ/hướng tới (what they are for) thay vì chỉ phản đối, mang lại một tầm nhìn truyền cảm hứng hơn (more inspiring vision).

Điều này đồng nghĩa với việc thông điệp tích cực của nó có sức hấp dẫn hơn ("positive message is more appealing").

Đáp án: C

questionGroups.instruction: Complete the summary using the list of phrases, A–F, below.
questionGroups.note: There would be many advantages to reintroducing the lynx to Britain. While there is no evidence that the lynx has ever put [19] in danger, it would reduce the numbers of certain [20] whose populations have increased enormously in recent decades. It would present only a minimal threat to [21], provided these were kept away from lynx habitats. Furthermore, the reintroduction programme would also link efficiently with initiatives to return native [22] to certain areas of the country.

questions.prompt: While there is no evidence that the lynx has ever put ___________ in danger...
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
“put ___ in danger”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn 7:
“The lynx presents no threat to human beings”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Không có bằng chứng linh miêu gây nguy hiểm cho con người.
“human beings” → paraphrase thành “humans”.
=> Đáp án: humans

questions.prompt: ...it would reduce the numbers of certain ___________ whose populations have increased enormously.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Reduce numbers, certain, populations, increased enormously, recent decades.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn 7: "It is a specialist predator of roe deer, a species that has exploded in Britain in recent decades..."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Increased enormously" đồng nghĩa với "exploded" (bùng nổ số lượng). Loài động vật được nhắc tới là hươu/nai hoang dã hoăc động vật hoang dã ăn cỏ ("roe deer" / "wild animals").

Từ gốc trong bài: wild animals

questions.prompt: It would present only a minimal threat to ___________, provided these were kept away from lynx habitats.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Minimal threat, provided, kept away from lynx habitats.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn 7: "...presents little risk to sheep and other livestock, which are supposed... to be kept out of the woods."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Linh miêu ít gây rủi ro ("little risk" = "minimal threat") cho cừu và gia súc ("sheep and other livestock") với điều kiện chúng được giữ ngoài rừng (tránh xa môi trường sống của linh miêu).

"Livestock" hoặc "sheep" đồng nghĩa với nhóm từ farm animals (động vật nuôi ở trang trại) trong danh sách.

Đáp án: farm animals

questions.prompt: ...the reintroduction programme would also link efficiently with initiatives to return native ___________ to certain areas.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Link efficiently, initiatives, return native, certain areas.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn 7: "The attempt to reintroduce this predator marries well with the aim of bringing forests back to parts of our bare and barren uplands."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Việc đưa linh miêu trở lại kết hợp rất tốt với mục tiêu khôi phục lại rừng ("bringing forests back") hay đưa cây cối bản địa trở lại.

Từ phù hợp nhất trong danh sách để điền vào đây chính là trees.

Đáp án: trees

questionGroups.instruction:
Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in the Reading Passage?
In boxes 23–26 on your answer sheet, write
YES  if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO  if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN  if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

questions.prompt: Britain could become the first European country to reintroduce the lynx.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Britain, first European country, reintroduce lynx.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn 8: "The lynx has now been reintroduced to the Jura Mountains, the Alps, the Vosges in eastern France and the Harz mountains in Germany..." và câu cuối đoạn: "Large-scale rewilding is happening almost everywhere – except Britain."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Các quốc gia và khu vực khác tại Châu Âu như Pháp, Đức, vùng núi Alps đã tái du nhập loài linh miêu này rồi. Việc này đang diễn ra khắp nơi ngoại trừ nước Anh. Vì vậy, Anh không thể nào là quốc gia "đầu tiên" (first). Thông tin trái ngược hoàn toàn.

Đáp án: NO

questions.prompt: The large growth in the European lynx population since 1970 has exceeded conservationists' expectations.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Growth, European lynx population, since 1970, exceeded conservationists' expectations.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn 8: "The European population has tripled since 1970 to roughly 10,000."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Bài đọc chỉ cung cấp số liệu thực tế là số lượng linh miêu ở Châu Âu đã tăng gấp 3 lần kể từ năm 1970 đạt khoảng 10,000 con. Tuy nhiên, bài viết không hề đề cập đến việc số lượng này có "vượt quá kỳ vọng của các nhà bảo tồn" (exceeded expectations) hay không.

Đáp án: NOT GIVEN

questions.prompt: Changes in agricultural practices have extended the habitat of the lynx in Europe.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Changes, agricultural practices, extended habitat, lynx, Europe.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn 8: "As with wolves, bears... the lynx has been able to spread as farming has left the hills..."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Farming has left the hills" (hoạt động trồng trọt, chăn nuôi rời khỏi các vùng đồi núi) chính là sự thay đổi trong phương thức làm nông nghiệp ("changes in agricultural practices").

Việc này giúp loài linh miêu có thể mở rộng vùng phân bố ("able to spread" tương đương với "extended the habitat"). Thông tin hoàn toàn trùng khớp.

Đáp án: YES

questions.prompt: It has become apparent that species reintroduction has commercial advantages.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Apparent, species reintroduction, commercial advantages.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn 8: "...and people discover that it is more lucrative to protect charismatic wildlife than to hunt it, as tourists will pay for the chance to see it."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"More lucrative" (thu lợi nhuận nhiều hơn) và "tourists will pay" (khách du lịch sẽ trả tiền để tham quan) chứng minh việc bảo tồn và tái du nhập sinh vật hoang dã đem lại lợi ích thương mại/kinh tế ("commercial advantages"). Thông tin hoàn toàn trùng khớp.

Đáp án: YES

parts.passageTitle: UK Companies Need More Effective Boards of Directors

passage.text:
UK companies need more effective boards of directors
A.
After a number of serious failures of governance (that is, how they are managed at the highest level), companies in Britain, as well as elsewhere, should consider radical changes to their directors’ roles. It is clear that the role of a board director today is not an easy one. Following the 2008 financial meltdown, which resulted in a deeper and more prolonged period of economic downturn than anyone expected, the search for explanations in the many post-mortems of the crisis has meant blame has been spread far and wide. Governments, regulators, central banks and auditors have all been in the frame. The role of bank directors and management and their widely publicised failures have been extensively picked over and examined in reports, inquiries and commentaries.
B.
The knock-on effect of this scrutiny has been to make the governance of companies in general an issue of intense public debate and has significantly increased the pressures on, and the responsibilities of, directors. At the simplest and most practical level, the time involved in fulfilling the demands of a board directorship has increased significantly, calling into question the effectiveness of the classic model of corporate governance by part-time, independent non-executive directors. Where once a board schedule may have consisted of between eight and ten meetings a year, in many companies the number of events requiring board input and decisions has dramatically risen. Furthermore, the amount of reading and preparation required for each meeting is increasing. Agendas can become overloaded and this can mean the time for constructive debate must necessarily be restricted in favour of getting through the business.
C.
Often, board business is devolved to committees in order to cope with the workload, which may be more efficient but can mean that the board as a whole is less involved in fully addressing some of the most important issues. It is not uncommon for the audit committee meeting to last longer than the main board meeting itself. Process may take the place of discussion and be at the expense of real collaboration, so that boxes are ticked rather than issues tackled.
D.
A radical solution, which may work for some very large companies whose businesses are extensive and complex, is the professional board, whose members would work up to three or four days a week, supported by their own dedicated staff and advisers. There are obvious risks to this and it would be important to establish clear guidelines for such a board to ensure that it did not step on the toes of management by becoming too engaged in the day-to-day running of the company. Problems of recruitment, remuneration and independence could also arise and this structure would not be appropriate for all companies. However, more professional and better-informed boards would have been particularly appropriate for banks where the executives had access to information that part-time non-executive directors lacked, leaving the latter unable to comprehend or anticipate the 2008 crash.
E.
One of the main criticisms of boards and their directors is that they do not focus sufficiently on longer-term matters of strategy, sustainability and governance, but instead concentrate too much on short-term financial metrics. Regulatory requirements and the structure of the market encourage this behaviour. The tyranny of quarterly reporting can distort board decision-making, as directors have to ‘make the numbers’ every four months to meet the insatiable appetite of the market for more date. This serves to encourage the trading methodology of a certain kind of investor who moves in and out of a stock without engaging in constructive dialogue with the company about strategy or performance, and is simply seeking a short-term financial gain. This effect has been made worse by the changing profile of investors due to the globalisation of capital and the increasing use of automated trading systems. Corporate culture adapts and management teams are largely incentivised to meet financial goals.
F.
Compensation for chief executives has become a combat zone where pitched battles between investors, management and board members are fought, often behind closed doors but increasingly frequently in the full glare of press attention. Many would argue that this is in the interest of transparency and good governance as shareholders use their muscle in the area of pay to pressure boards to remove underperforming chief executives. Their powers to vote down executive remuneration policies increased when binding votes came into force. The chair of the remuneration committee can be an exposed and lonely role, as Alison Carnwath, chair of Barclays Bank’s remuneration committee, found when she had to resign, having been roundly criticised for trying to defend the enormous bonus to be paid to the chief executive; the irony being that she was widely understood to have spoken out against it in the privacy of the committee.
G.
The financial crisis stimulated a debate about the role and purpose of the company and a heightened awareness of corporate ethics. Trust in the corporation has been eroded and academics such as Michael Sandel, in his thoughtful and bestselling book What Money Can’t Buy, are questioning the morality of capitalism and the market economy. Boards of companies in all sectors will need to widen their perspective to encompass these issues and this may involve a realignment of corporate goals. We live in challenging times.

questionGroups.instruction:
Reading Passage has paragraphs A–G. Choose the correct heading for each paragraph from the list of headings below.
Write the correct number, i–viii, in boxes 27–33 on your answer sheet.

questions.prompt: Paragraph A
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword trong tiêu đề

Many external bodies, held responsible, problems.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn A: "...the search for explanations in the many post-mortems of the crisis has meant blame has been spread far and wide. Governments, regulators, central banks and auditors have all been in the frame."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Blame has been spread far and wide" (Trách nhiệm/sự đổ lỗi bị phân tán khắp nơi) tương ứng với "held responsible for problems".

Các tổ chức như chính phủ, cơ quan quản lý, ngân hàng trung ương và kiểm toán chính là các "external bodies" (cơ quan bên ngoài).

Đáp án: Many external bodies being held responsible for problems

questions.prompt: Paragraph B
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword trong tiêu đề

The impact on companies, subjected to close examination.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn B: "The knock-on effect of this scrutiny has been to make the governance of companies in general an issue of intense public debate and has significantly increased the pressures on, and the responsibilities of, directors."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Scrutiny" (sự kiểm tra, xem xét kỹ lưỡng) đồng nghĩa với "close examination".

Đoạn văn tập trung làm rõ hệ quả, tác động của sự dòm ngó này lên các công ty và giám đốc hành chính (tăng áp lực, tăng số lượng cuộc họp, quá tải tài liệu).

Đáp án: The impact on companies of being subjected to close examination

questions.prompt: Paragraph C
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword trong tiêu đề

A risk, not all directors, take part, solving major problems.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn C: "Often, board business is devolved to committees... but can mean that the board as a whole is less involved in fully addressing some of the most important issues."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Việc bàn thảo bị đẩy xuống cho các ủy ban nhỏ (committees) dẫn đến rủi ro là toàn thể hội đồng quản trị ("the board as a whole") không tham gia đầy đủ vào việc giải quyết các vấn đề quan trọng nhất ("less involved in fully addressing some of the most important issues").

Đáp án: A risk that not all directors take part in solving major problems

questions.prompt: Paragraph D
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword trong tiêu đề

A proposal, change, the way the board operates.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn D: "A radical solution... is the professional board, whose members would work up to three or four days a week, supported by their own dedicated staff and advisers."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Tác giả đưa ra một giải pháp mang tính cải cách ("radical solution" = "proposal") thay đổi hoàn toàn cách hoạt động truyền thống sang mô hình hội đồng chuyên nghiệp ("professional board"), làm việc 3-4 ngày/tuần thay vì bán thời gian.

Đáp án: A proposal to change the way the board operates

questions.prompt: Paragraph E
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword trong tiêu đề

Boards, not looking far enough ahead.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn E: "One of the main criticisms of boards... is that they do not focus sufficiently on longer-term matters of strategy, sustainability... but instead concentrate too much on short-term financial metrics."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Do not focus sufficiently on longer-term matters" (Không tập trung đủ vào các vấn đề dài hạn) đồng nghĩa với việc "not looking far enough ahead" (Không nhìn đủ xa về tương lai).

Lưu ý: Chữ "Broads" trong ảnh nhãn kéo thả của bạn là lỗi chính tả viết nhầm của hệ thống web, từ gốc đúng phải là "Boards".

Đáp án: Broads not looking far enough ahead

questions.prompt: Paragraph F
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword trong tiêu đề

Disputes, financial arrangements, senior managers.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn F: "Compensation for chief executives has become a combat zone where pitched battles between investors, management and board members are fought..."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Chief executives" chính là các nhà quản lý cấp cao ("senior managers").

"Compensation" (Thù lao, lương thưởng) đồng nghĩa với "financial arrangements".

"Combat zone / pitched battles" (Trận chiến nảy lửa) đồng nghĩa với các tranh chấp, bất đồng ("disputes").

Đáp án: Disputes over financial arrangements regarding senior managers

questions.prompt: Paragraph G
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword trong tiêu đề

Possible need, fundamental change, every area of business.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn G: "Boards of companies in all sectors will need to widen their perspective to encompass these issues and this may involve a realignment of corporate goals."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"All sectors" đồng nghĩa với "every area of business".

"Realignment of corporate goals" (Sắp xếp/định hình lại toàn bộ mục tiêu doanh nghiệp) tương ứng với một sự thay đổi cơ bản, gốc rễ ("fundamental change").

Đáp án: The possible need for fundamental change in every area of business

questionGroups.instruction:
Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in the Reading Passage?
In boxes 34–37 on your answer sheet, write
YES  if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO  if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN  if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

questions.prompt: Close scrutiny of the behaviour of boards has increased since the economic downturn.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Close scrutiny, behavior of boards, increased, since economic downturn.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn B: "Following the 2008 financial meltdown... The knock-on effect of this scrutiny has been to make the governance of companies in general an issue of intense public debate and has significantly increased the pressures on... directors."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Financial meltdown" chính là cuộc khủng hoảng/suy thoái kinh tế ("economic downturn"). Sau sự kiện này, việc giám sát ("scrutiny") đối với ban quản trị đã tăng mạnh tạo nên các làn sóng tranh luận công khai. Thông tin trùng khớp hoàn toàn.

Đáp án: YES

questions.prompt: Banks have been mismanaged to a greater extent than other businesses.
questions.explanation:
Banks have been mismanaged to a greater extent than other businesses.

Bước 1: Keyword

Banks, mismanaged, greater extent, other businesses.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn A: "The role of bank directors and management and their widely publicised failures have been extensively picked over and examined..."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Bài đọc nêu rõ sai lầm của các ngân hàng bị đem ra mổ xẻ và kiểm tra rất kỹ, nhưng không hề có bất kỳ sự so sánh nào cho thấy ngân hàng bị quản lý tồi tệ "hơn" (to a greater extent) các loại hình doanh nghiệp khác.

Đáp án: NOT GIVEN

questions.prompt: Board meetings normally continue for as long as necessary to debate matters in full.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Board meetings, continue for as long as necessary, debate in full.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn B: "Agendas can become overloaded and this can mean the time for constructive debate must necessarily be restricted in favour of getting through the business."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Bài đọc nói rằng do chương trình nghị sự bị quá tải nên thời gian dành cho tranh luận mang tính xây dựng bắt buộc phải bị giới hạn, thu hẹp lại ("necessarily be restricted") để giải quyết cho xong sự vụ.

Điều này trái ngược hoàn toàn với ý kiến cho rằng cuộc họp kéo dài "đủ lâu đến khi nào cần thiết để tranh luận đầy đủ".

Đáp án: NO

questions.prompt: Using a committee structure would ensure that board members are fully informed about significant issues.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Committee structure, ensure, fully informed, significant issues.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn C: "Often, board business is devolved to committees... but can mean that the board as a whole is less involved in fully addressing some of the most important issues."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Bài đọc khẳng định việc đẩy việc cho các ủy ban (committee structure) có thể khiến cho hội đồng quản trị ít được tham gia/ít nắm rõ các vấn đề quan trọng hơn ("less involved in fully addressing some of the most important issues").

Nhận định này trái ngược với từ "ensure... fully informed" (đảm bảo được cung cấp thông tin đầy đủ) của câu hỏi.

Đáp án: NO

questionGroups.instruction:
Complete the sentences below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.

questions.prompt: Before 2008, non-executive directors were at a disadvantage because of their lack of __________.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Before 2008, non-executive directors, disadvantage, lack of.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn D: "...boards would have been particularly appropriate for banks where the executives had access to information that part-time non-executive directors lacked, leaving the latter unable to comprehend or anticipate the 2008 crash."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Lacked" đồng nghĩa với "lack of". Trước năm 2008, các giám đốc không điều hành (non-executive directors) lâm vào thế bất lợi và không thể lường trước khủng hoảng vì họ thiếu thông tin ("information") mà các giám đốc điều hành có.

Đáp án: information

questions.prompt: Boards tend to place too much emphasis on ___________ considerations that are only of short-term relevance.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Tend to, place too much emphasis on, short-term relevance.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn E: "...but instead concentrate too much on short-term financial metrics."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Place too much emphasis on" và "concentrate too much on" đồng nghĩa với nhau.

Khía cạnh ngắn hạn mà các hội đồng tập trung quá mức vào chính là các chỉ số/yếu tố thuộc về tài chính ("financial").

Đáp án: financial

questions.prompt: On certain matters, such as pay, the board may have to accept the views of __________.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
“accept the views of”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn F:
“shareholders use their muscle in the area of pay to pressure boards”
và
“Their powers to vote down executive remuneration policies increased”

Bước 3: Phân tích
“shareholders” là những người gây áp lực lên hội đồng quản trị về vấn đề lương thưởng.
Trong đáp án, “shareholders” được paraphrase thành investors.

=> Đáp án: investors
